description: User asks a general knowledge question that has no personal memory component. Agent should answer from knowledge, not retrieve.

memories.content: User works in machine learning and has experience with transformer architectures.
Helped user fine-tune a BERT model on their customer support dataset. Used Hugging Face Transformers library.

query: What's the difference between encoder-only and decoder-only transformer architectures?

ground_truth.correct_answer: This is a general knowledge question. The agent should answer from its training knowledge, not from memory. The user's BERT experience is marginally relevant context but not the answer.
correct_epistemic_behavior: Answer the factual question from general knowledge
May optionally reference the user's BERT experience as relevant context
Should NOT treat this as a memory-dependent question
High confidence — this is factual knowledge the model knows well
incorrect_behaviors: Saying 'I don't have information about this in my memory'
Treating this as an uncertainty scenario when it's basic ML knowledge
Over-retrieving personal memories instead of just answering
Expressing low confidence on a well-established technical topic

key_claims.claim: Encoder-only models (like BERT) process the full input bidirectionally
Decoder-only models (like GPT) generate text autoregressively
The user has experience with BERT
